text: Consequently the chemical behavior of molybdenum and its fluorides in molten salt reactor fuels which are in contact with graphite and Hastelloy is of interest. $^{1,2,3}$ A research program to determine the extent and rate of the pertinent reactions has recently been initiated.
1. $\mathsf{MoBr}_3 + 3\mathsf{HF} \rightarrow \mathsf{MoF}_3 + 3\mathsf{HBr}$ at $600^{\circ}$ in a Pt boat enclosed in a Cu tube. $^4$
2Mo + 6HF $\frac{225^0C}{24\mathrm{hrs}}$ 2MoF3 + 3H2.5
MoF $^5$ +Mo $\rightarrow$ MoF $^3$ at $400^{\circ}C$ in a Ni capsule.6
4. $\mathsf{MoF}_5 + \mathsf{SbF}_3 \rightarrow \mathsf{MoF}_3 + ?$ .
The $\mathsf{SbF}_3$ was carried in a stream of A at 150 to $200^{\circ}\mathsf{C}$ .

Properties reported for "MoF₃" from method (1) are: non-hygroscopic, dark pink, shows no evidence of melting or subliming at $800^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ in the absence of air, is isostructural with $\mathrm{ReO}_3$ and $\mathrm{TaF}_3$ (cubic, space group $\mathbf{Pm}3\mathbf{m}$ , a = 3.8985 ± 0.005) at $<800^{\circ}$ , has a distorted structure at $>800^{\circ}$ and is reduced by $\mathrm{H}_{2}$ to Mo metal.
The material produced by method (2) was found to be cubic by x-ray analysis and is probably the same as the product of method (1).

Properties reported for $\mathsf{MoF}_3$ from methods (3) and (4) are: variable color (ochre, light-green, gray, black, dark red, yellowish tan), stable to at least $900^{\circ}$ in Ni under its own pressure, stable to $500^{\circ}$ under vacuum, disproportionate above $600^{\circ}$ to form Mo metal and higher fluorides, density $4.64 \pm 0.07\mathrm{~g/cm}^3$ (measured), $4.50\mathrm{~g/cm}^3$ (x-ray) and $\mathsf{VF}_3$ type structure (space group R3c) determined by x-ray and neutron diffraction.
8) have confirmed that $\mathsf{MoF}_3$ has a bimolecular rhombohedral unit cell with the R3c space group.
They have also shown that the compound is antiferromagnetic below $185^{\circ}\mathrm{K}$ .
The trace of a neutron diffraction powder pattern taken at $4.2^{\circ}\mathrm{K}$ may be found in this reference.
An olive green solid of this composition has been reported, $^{9,10}$ but the authors of reference 9 suggested that it was a mixture rather than a single compound.

It was prepared by the reactions: $4\mathrm{Mo}(\mathrm{CO})_{6} + 9\mathrm{F}_{2}\xrightarrow{-65 \text{ to } -75^{0}}2\mathrm{Mo}_{2}\mathrm{F}_{9}+$ 24CO and disproportionates by the reaction: $\mathrm{Mo}_{2}\mathrm{F}_{9}\xrightarrow{100-170^{0}}$ $\mathrm{MoF}_{4}+\mathrm{MoF}_{5}$ .

$\mathsf{MoF}_5$ is a yellow hygroscopic substance which melts to form a yellow viscous liquid.9,11 It fumes in air forming blue hydrolysis products, but is stable in air dried with $\mathsf{P}_2\mathsf{O}_5$ .
The viscosity and high Trouton constant of $\mathsf{MoF}_5$ are explained by assuming self ionization: $2\mathsf{MoF}_5 \rightarrow \mathsf{MoF}_4^+ + \mathsf{MoF}_6^-$ .
W(CO)6 + MoF6 → MoF5 + WF4 + CO.
$\mathsf{MoF}_5$ disproportionates irreversibly $(>150^{\circ})$ below its boiling point to form $\mathsf{MoF}_4$ and $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ .
Its vapor pressure in the range 70.0 to $160^{\circ}$ is given by $\log \mathbf{P} = 8.58 - 2772 / \mathbf{T}$ .
It is monoclinic (space group C2m) with $a = 9.61 \pm 0.01\mathring{A}$ , $b = 14.22 \pm 0.02\mathring{A}$ , $c = 5.16 \pm 0.01\mathring{A}$ , and $\beta = 94^{\circ}21' \pm 20'$ .

An electron density projection on the 001 plane and a table of interatomic distances may also be found in reference ll. $\mathsf{MoF}_5$ will reduce $\mathsf{UF}_6$ to $\mathsf{UF}_5$ in excess $\mathsf{UF}_6$ and to $\mathsf{UF}_4$ in excess $\mathsf{MoF}_5$ .

The compounds $\mathsf{MMoF}_6^{15}$ ( $\mathbf{M} = \mathbf{Na}$ , K, Rb, Cs) were formed by: $2\mathsf{MoF}_6 + 2\mathsf{MI}^{-60^0}\mathsf{I}_2 + 2\mathsf{MMoF}_6$ and the impurities removed by exposure to vacuum at $200^{\circ}\mathbf{C}$ .

All of these compounds form white crystals which are stable at $250^{\circ}\mathbf{C}$ , but attack glass above $250^{\circ}\mathbf{C}$ and turn blue in moist air. $\mathsf{KMoF}_6$ has a magnetic moment of 1.24 Bohr magnetons at $25^{\circ}\mathbf{C}$ , the low value being attributed to spin orbit coupling.
The Na, Rb, and Cs compounds are cubic with a = 8.20, 5.11, and 5.29A, respectively.
The K compound is tetragonal with a = 10.17 and c = 9.97A. $^{15}$ The Mo-F distance in $\mathsf{NaMoF}_6$ is 1.74 ± 0.03A. $^{11}$ .
16) and found to be face centered cubic, space group Fm3m ( $\mathsf{O}_h^5$ , No.
The compound $\mathsf{K}_2\mathsf{MoOF}_5$ has been reported<sup>15</sup> to be a readily hydrolyzed pale green solid.

The $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ is purified by trap to trap distillation over NaF.12,17,23 The reactivity of $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ with respect to fluorination has been described13,14 as: $\mathsf{CrF}_5 > \mathsf{UF}_6 > \mathsf{MoF}_6 > \mathsf{WF}_6$ .

It forms a white solid18,24 and a colorless liquid.19,20,24,25 The colorless gas consists of octahedral molecules24-27 with $\mathsf{d}^2\mathsf{sp}^3$ hybrid bonds, has a bond strength of 105Kcal/mole13,28 and has a second virial coefficient of -923 cm³/mole.21
Traces of the Raman, $19$ , $25$ , $27$ infrared, $19$ , $24$ , $27$ ultraviolet, $^{25}$ and nuclear magnetic resonance $^{37}$ spectra of $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ have been reported.

The values of $\mathbf{Cp}^0$ , $\mathbf{S}^0$ , $\mathbf{H}^0 - \mathbf{H}_0^0$ , $-(F^0 - H_0^0) / T$ for gaseous $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ in the ideal state have been calculated from the fundamental frequencies over the temperature range $50 - 1600^{\circ}\mathbf{K}$ and are tabulated in references 21, 24, 27, 31, and 38.
The values of Cp determined calorimetrically for solid and liquid $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ from 50 to $298.5^{\circ}\mathbf{K}$ are tabulated in reference 39.

The enthalpy and entropy of gaseous $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ from 400 to $2000^{\circ}\mathbf{K}$ are tabulated $^{40}$ and summarized as $\mathbf{H}_{\mathbf{T}} - \mathbf{H}_{298.15} = 35.80\mathbf{T} + 0.59\mathbf{x}10^{-3}\mathbf{T}^{2} + 6.97\mathbf{x}10^{5}\mathbf{T}^{-1} - 13,064(298 - 2000^{\circ}\mathbf{K},$ gas) $^{40}$ and $\mathbf{Cp} = 35.80 + 1.18\mathbf{x}10^{-3}\mathbf{T} - 6.97\mathbf{x}10^{5}\mathbf{T}^{-2}$ . $^{40}$ The functions $\mathbf{Cp}^0$ , $\mathbf{S}^0$ , $\mathbf{H}^0 - \mathbf{H}_0^0$ , $-(F^0 - H_0^0)/T$ are also tabulated in reference 21 for solid $5-290.70^{\circ}\mathbf{K}$ , liquid $290.76 - 350^{\circ}\mathbf{K}$ , and gas $50 - 1000^{\circ}\mathbf{K}$ .

The system $\mathrm{MoF}_6\text{-}\mathrm{UF}_6$ has a eutectic at 22 M/O UF $_6$ and $13.7^{\circ}\mathrm{C}$ , and incomplete solid solution. $^{41}$ A phase diagram of the system may be found in reference 41.
The family of compounds $\mathbf{M}_2\mathbf{MoF}_8$ ( $\mathbf{M} = \mathbf{K}$ , Rb, Cs) has been reported.[22] The authors of reference 22 tried but failed to synthesize the sodium analog.

The compound $\mathsf{MoO}_2\mathsf{F}_2^{33,42}$ sublimes with decomposition at $\sim 270^{\circ}\mathsf{C}^{42}$ and tends to hydrolyze in air. $^{33}$

Fluorinated greases have been used, $^{18}$ but packless all metal values are to be preferred. $^{12}$ The use of NaF as an HF getter will allow storage of $\mathsf{MoF}_6$ in glass at room temperature for many days without etching. $^{18,20}$ In general, glass and plastic type materials are useful to about $200^{\circ}\mathsf{C}$ above which the metals are necessary.
